mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and slowing output, which suppresses earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the regime-specific alpha vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase above 0.4% triggers immediate inflation fears, prompting rapid portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or mean returns are negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth during a post-shock recovery phase signals pent-up demand fulfillment and supply chain normalization, driving immediate positive S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO surges during recovery periods fail to generate positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when market volatility exceeds 20%.
